04-health-records.summary: Australia operates My Health Record, a national digital health record system managed by the Australian Digital Health Agency. The system provides a centralized, opt-out repository of health information including Medicare claims, prescriptions, pathology reports, diagnostic imaging, hospital discharge summaries, and vaccination records. Citizens can access their records online or via the myGov portal. Healthcare providers can view and contribute to records through clinical software integrated with the My Health Record system.
04-health-records.notes: My Health Record transitioned to an opt-out model in 2019, resulting in approximately 90% of Australians having a record. The system is supplemented by electronic prescribing (ePrescribing) and secure messaging between healthcare providers. The Australian Immunisation Register (AIR) is fully integrated, providing comprehensive vaccination records.

12-justice-legal-aid.summary: Australia provides some digital justice and legal aid services, but availability varies significantly across states and territories. Legal Aid commissions in several states offer online eligibility checkers and application forms. Some courts have introduced electronic filing, but the level of digitization is inconsistent across jurisdictions.
12-justice-legal-aid.notes: The Federal Court offers eLodgment for electronic court filing. State-level legal aid organizations (e.g., Legal Aid NSW, Victoria Legal Aid) have varying degrees of online service. The Commonwealth Courts Portal provides case search and some digital filing capabilities.

15-immigration-travel.summary: Australia provides comprehensive digital immigration and travel services through ImmiAccount for online visa applications and management, the Australian Passport Office's online renewal service, and SmartGates for automated border processing at airports. The majority of visa subclasses can be applied for and managed entirely online.
15-immigration-travel.notes: Australia was an early adopter of electronic travel authorization (ETA) and ePassport technology. The Digital Passenger Declaration replaced the previous incoming passenger card. VEVO (Visa Entitlement Verification Online) allows employers and organizations to check visa status digitally.

02-civil-registration.summary: Bangladesh has deployed OpenCRVS, an open-source civil registration and vital statistics platform, to digitize birth and death registration nationwide. The system enables registration at union digital centres across the country, with records linked to the national identity system. Bangladesh was the first country to deploy OpenCRVS at national scale, and the system has significantly improved registration completeness and timeliness.

sources.title: Office of the Registrar General, Birth and Death Registration

02-civil-registration.notes: OpenCRVS was developed in partnership with Plan International and the Government of Bangladesh. It is recognized as a Digital Public Good by the DPGA. Birth registration completeness has improved substantially since the system's deployment.

01-digital-identity.summary: Brazil provides digital identity through the CPF (Cadastro de Pessoas Fisicas) system and the GOV.BR platform. The GOV.BR single sign-on portal enables citizens to access over 4,000 federal government services with tiered identity verification levels (bronze, silver, gold). The platform integrates facial biometric verification and supports digital document storage. Brazil is also rolling out the new Carteira de Identidade Nacional (CIN), a unified national ID card replacing fragmented state-level documents.

sources.title: GOV.BR - Portal de Servicos do Governo Federal

01-digital-identity.notes: GOV.BR has over 150 million registered accounts. The platform uses a tiered trust model: bronze (basic CPF verification), silver (facial biometrics or bank validation), and gold (biometric certificate). The CIN rollout, which unifies state-level identity documents under a single national standard linked to the CPF, is ongoing.

03-digital-payments.summary: Brazil operates PIX, an instant payment system launched by the Central Bank of Brazil (Banco Central do Brasil) in November 2020. PIX enables free, real-time transfers 24/7 between individuals, businesses, and government entities. With over 150 million registered users, PIX has become the most-used payment method in Brazil. The government uses PIX for tax refunds, social benefit disbursements (including Bolsa Familia), and fee collection across federal, state, and municipal services.
03-digital-payments.notes: PIX processed over 4 billion transactions in its first month of reaching full adoption and continues to grow rapidly. It has largely displaced boleto bancario (payment slips) and wire transfers for everyday transactions. PIX is credited with significantly advancing financial inclusion in Brazil, particularly among lower-income populations.

06-social-protection.summary: Brazil operates one of the world's largest conditional cash transfer programs through Bolsa Familia (relaunched in 2023 after the Auxilio Brasil period). The Cadastro Unico (Unified Registry) digitally registers over 90 million low-income individuals, enabling enrollment across multiple social programs. Payments are delivered through Caixa Economica Federal's digital banking infrastructure, including the Caixa Tem app. The system supports digital enrollment, eligibility verification, and benefit tracking.

sources.title: Bolsa Familia - Ministerio do Desenvolvimento e Assistencia Social

06-social-protection.notes: Bolsa Familia reaches over 21 million families. The Cadastro Unico serves as the gateway for over 25 federal social programs. The Caixa Tem app has been instrumental in digitizing benefit payments, particularly accelerated during the COVID-19 emergency aid distribution.

09-education-platform.summary: Brazil's Ministry of Education (MEC) provides several digital education platforms. ProUni (University for All Program) manages scholarship applications online, while SISU (Unified Selection System) handles public university admissions digitally based on ENEM exam scores. The INEP (National Institute for Educational Studies and Research) manages digital assessments and education statistics. The Plataforma MEC RED offers open educational resources. However, significant disparities exist between urban and rural areas, and many public schools lack adequate digital infrastructure.
09-education-platform.notes: Brazil's ENEM (National High School Exam) is one of the world's largest standardized tests, with over 4 million annual participants registering digitally. The e-MEC system provides accreditation and regulatory information for all higher education institutions. COVID-19 exposed significant gaps in digital readiness, particularly in northern and northeastern states. The Escola Conectada (Connected School) program aims to bring broadband to all public schools.

10-business-registration.summary: Brazil has implemented the Redesim (National Network for the Simplification of Registration and Legalization of Businesses) to streamline business registration across federal, state, and municipal levels. The Redesim portal integrates CNPJ registration (federal tax ID), state commercial registry (Junta Comercial), and municipal licensing into a single digital entry point. For low-risk activities, registration can be completed online with automatic approval. However, the experience varies significantly by municipality, with some still requiring in-person steps.
10-business-registration.notes: The MEI (Microempreendedor Individual) registration is fully digital and free, enabling informal workers to formalize quickly. Over 15 million MEIs are registered. For larger company types, the process remains more complex due to the need for state and municipal approvals. The Digital Government Strategy has accelerated integration, but full harmonization across 5,570 municipalities remains a work in progress. Processing times range from same-day for MEIs to several weeks in some municipalities.

14-public-procurement.summary: Colombia provides comprehensive digital procurement through SECOP II (Sistema Electronico de Contratacion Publica), managed by Colombia Compra Eficiente. SECOP II supports end-to-end transactional e-procurement including planning, tendering, evaluation, and contract management. Colombia is a leading adopter of the Open Contracting Data Standard (OCDS).
14-public-procurement.notes: Colombia was one of the first countries to fully adopt OCDS for procurement data publication, enabling transparency and analytics. SECOP II replaced the earlier SECOP I (information-only) platform with full transactional capabilities. The Tienda Virtual del Estado Colombiano provides a digital marketplace for framework agreements.

01-digital-identity.summary: Estonia operates one of the world's most advanced digital identity systems. Every citizen and resident holds a mandatory national ID card with a chip enabling cryptographic authentication and digital signatures. The system supports e-Residency, allowing non-residents to establish a trusted digital identity for accessing Estonian e-services. The ID card is used for voting, healthcare access, banking, tax filing, and over 99% of government services.
01-digital-identity.notes: Estonia's ID card infrastructure relies on public key infrastructure (PKI) managed by SK ID Solutions. Mobile-ID and Smart-ID provide alternative digital identity verification methods. The e-Residency programme has enrolled over 100,000 e-residents from 170+ countries.

04-health-records.summary: Estonia operates a comprehensive e-Health system where patients can view their complete medical records online through the Patient Portal (digilugu.ee). The system aggregates data from all healthcare providers into a unified national health record, including diagnoses, prescriptions, lab results, imaging, and vaccination history. Healthcare providers access records through the Health Information System (TIS), which exchanges data securely via X-Road infrastructure.
04-health-records.notes: Estonia's e-Health system has been operational since 2008. 99% of health data is digitized, and e-Prescriptions account for 99% of all prescriptions issued. The system uses blockchain technology (KSI) to ensure data integrity. Patients can control access to their records and see which providers have accessed their data.

05-population-registry.summary: Estonia's Population Register (Rahvastikuregister) is a fully digital, centralized database maintained by the Ministry of the Interior. It contains records of all Estonian citizens, residents, and e-residents, including personal data, addresses, family relations, citizenship, and vital events. The register is the authoritative source for personal data across all government systems, accessible in real-time via X-Road data exchange infrastructure. Citizens can view and update their data through the eesti.ee portal.
05-population-registry.notes: The Population Register operates on a once-only principle: citizens provide data once and it is reused across all government services via X-Road. The register is linked to the e-Identity system for authentication. Real-time updates ensure that address changes, births, deaths, and marriages are reflected immediately across all connected systems.

07-tax-administration.summary: Estonia's e-Tax Board (e-MTA) is one of the most advanced digital tax systems globally. Pre-filled tax returns auto-populate income, deductions, and credits from employer and financial institution data, allowing most citizens to complete annual filing in under five minutes. The system handles personal income tax, corporate tax, VAT, and social tax declarations entirely online. Over 98% of tax returns are filed electronically.
07-tax-administration.notes: Estonia pioneered pre-filled tax returns in the early 2000s. The e-Tax system is integrated with the national X-Road data exchange platform, enabling real-time data sharing between government agencies. Authentication uses the national ID card, Mobile-ID, or Smart-ID. The system serves as a model for digital tax administration worldwide.

10-business-registration.summary: Estonia's e-Business Register provides a fully digital lifecycle for business registration and management. Companies can be established online in under 3 hours using a digital ID, with no need for in-person visits or notarization for standard company types. The Commercial Register is integrated with the national X-Road data exchange layer, enabling automatic data sharing with tax, employment, and statistics agencies. E-Residency holders can also register and manage Estonian companies entirely remotely from anywhere in the world.
10-business-registration.notes: Estonia's e-Business Register is often cited as a global best practice for digital business registration. The E-Residency programme has attracted over 100,000 e-residents from 170+ countries, many of whom register Estonian companies. Annual reports can be pre-filled with data from government databases. The system supports digital signatures for all corporate documents and shareholder decisions.

11-electoral-services.summary: Estonia is a global pioneer in digital electoral services, having introduced legally binding internet voting (i-Voting) in 2005. Citizens can vote online using their national ID card or Mobile-ID, register and verify their voter status digitally, and access election results in real time through the Estonian Election Commission portal.
11-electoral-services.notes: In the 2023 parliamentary elections, over 50% of votes were cast online. Estonia's i-Voting system is built on the national digital identity infrastructure (eID) and uses end-to-end verifiability.

12-justice-legal-aid.summary: Estonia offers fully digital justice services through the e-File system, which allows citizens and legal professionals to file court documents, track case progress, and access court decisions online. Legal aid applications can be submitted digitally, and the Public E-File portal provides public access to court information.
12-justice-legal-aid.notes: Estonia's e-File system has been operational since 2009 and is integrated with the national digital identity infrastructure. Court hearings can be conducted via video conference. The system covers civil, criminal, and administrative proceedings.

15-immigration-travel.summary: Estonia offers the pioneering e-Residency programme, allowing non-residents to access Estonian digital services for business purposes. Visa applications and residence permit processes are partially digitized through the Police and Border Guard Board's online services. However, full end-to-end digital visa application processing is still being developed.

sources.title: Police and Border Guard Board - Visa and Residence

15-immigration-travel.notes: Estonia's e-Residency programme has issued over 100,000 e-Residency cards to citizens of 170+ countries. For traditional immigration, some application steps still require in-person visits. Estonia participates in the EU's common visa policy and is implementing the EU Entry/Exit System (EES).

02-civil-registration.summary: Ethiopia established its civil registration and vital statistics (CRVS) system through the Vital Events Registration Agency (VERA), created in 2016. The system is being developed with support from UNICEF and other international partners. While the legal framework is in place and registration offices have been established at kebele (local) level across the country, coverage remains incomplete, particularly in pastoral and remote areas. Digitization of records is in early stages.
02-civil-registration.notes: Ethiopia only established mandatory civil registration in 2016, making it one of the last countries in Africa to do so. Birth registration rates have improved but remain below 10% for under-five children in some regions. The government's CRVS digitization strategy is being implemented with support from UNICEF, the World Bank, and Bloomberg Philanthropies' Data for Health Initiative.

13-agricultural-extension.summary: Ethiopia utilizes digital agricultural extension primarily through Digital Green's video-based extension approach, which trains development agents and farmers using locally produced instructional videos. The government's Agricultural Transformation Institute (ATI) supports digital initiatives, but internet penetration and smartphone access remain significant barriers to broader digital extension coverage.
13-agricultural-extension.notes: Digital Green is recognized as a Digital Public Good (DPG) by the DPGA. Ethiopia has one of the largest agricultural extension workforces in the world with over 60,000 development agents, but digital tools supplement rather than replace in-person extension. The 8028 IVR-based agricultural hotline provides phone-based advisory services.

05-population-registry.summary: Finland operates the Population Information System (Vaestotietojarjestelma, VTJ), maintained by the Digital and Population Data Services Agency (Digi- ja vaestotietovirasto, DVV). The system is a comprehensive digital register containing data on all Finnish citizens and permanent residents, including personal identity codes, names, addresses, family relationships, citizenship, and native language. The register serves as the authoritative data source for public administration, elections, taxation, and statistical purposes.

sources.title: Digital and Population Data Services Agency (DVV)

05-population-registry.notes: Finland's population register dates back to church parish records from the 1600s and has been fully digitized since the 1970s. The system assigns a personal identity code (henkilotunnus) to every person registered in Finland. DVV also maintains the Building and Dwelling Register. The system supports the Suomi.fi e-service platform for citizen-facing digital government services.

08-land-property-registry.summary: Georgia's National Agency of Public Registry (NAPR) provides fully digital property registration services, widely recognized as a global best practice. Property registration can be completed in under one day, with the online portal enabling title searches, application submissions, and status tracking. The system consolidated previously fragmented registries into a single digital platform. Georgia consistently ranks among the top countries globally in the World Bank's ease of registering property indicators.

sources.title: National Agency of Public Registry - Georgia

08-land-property-registry.notes: Georgia's property registration reform is considered one of the most successful in the post-Soviet region. Registration can be completed in 1 day for standard cases or 1-4 hours for expedited service. The NAPR also manages the civil registry and business registry. The system supports electronic signatures and provides public access to property records through the online cadastral map.

14-public-procurement.summary: Georgia operates a fully electronic public procurement system through the State Procurement Agency (SPA). The e-procurement portal supports electronic tendering, reverse auctions, and simplified procurement for low-value purchases. All public procurement above defined thresholds must be conducted through the electronic system, ensuring transparency and competition.

sources.title: State Procurement Agency of Georgia - E-Procurement

14-public-procurement.notes: Georgia's e-procurement system was introduced in 2010 and has been credited with reducing procurement costs and corruption. The system supports multiple procurement methods including electronic tenders, simplified electronic tenders, and consolidated tenders. Georgia publishes procurement data openly and has been recognized for transparency reforms.

01-digital-identity.summary: India operates Aadhaar, the world's largest biometric digital identity system, with over 1.3 billion enrolled individuals. Managed by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI), Aadhaar provides a 12-digit unique identity number linked to biometric and demographic data. It underpins access to government subsidies, banking, mobile services, and tax filing. The underlying technology is based on MOSIP (Modular Open Source Identity Platform), which has since been adopted by other countries.

sources.title: MOSIP - Modular Open Source Identity Platform

01-digital-identity.notes: Aadhaar is deeply integrated into India's digital public infrastructure stack (India Stack), alongside UPI for payments and DigiLocker for document storage. MOSIP is recognized as a Digital Public Good by the DPGA.

02-civil-registration.summary: India's Civil Registration System (CRS) is administered by the Registrar General of India under the Registration of Births and Deaths Act, 1969. While the legal framework mandates registration across all states and union territories, the level of digitization varies significantly by state. Some states like Kerala and Tamil Nadu have fully digital systems, while others still rely heavily on paper-based processes. The national birth and death registration portal enables online registration in participating states.

sources.title: Office of the Registrar General & Census Commissioner, India
Registration of Births and Deaths in India - Annual Report

02-civil-registration.notes: India's birth registration completeness has improved to approximately 93% nationally, but death registration lags behind at around 86%. The 2023 amendment to the Registration of Births and Deaths Act mandates a centralized digital database and links birth/death registration to Aadhaar, electoral rolls, and other national databases. Implementation of this amendment is ongoing across states.

03-digital-payments.summary: India operates the Unified Payments Interface (UPI), one of the world's most successful real-time digital payment systems. Developed by the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI), UPI processes over 10 billion transactions monthly, enabling instant bank-to-bank transfers via mobile phones. The system is deeply integrated into government payment infrastructure, including Direct Benefit Transfers (DBT), tax payments, and utility bills. UPI is interoperable across banks and payment apps including PhonePe, Google Pay, and Paytm.
03-digital-payments.notes: UPI launched in 2016 and has grown exponentially, becoming a core component of India Stack. International expansion is underway, with UPI-based payments now accepted in Singapore, UAE, France, and other countries. The system has been instrumental in financial inclusion, bringing hundreds of millions of previously unbanked citizens into the digital economy.

04-health-records.summary: India is rolling out the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), which aims to create a unified digital health ecosystem. The system assigns a 14-digit Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) number to citizens, enabling the creation of longitudinal digital health records. ABDM facilitates interoperability between hospitals, clinics, labs, and pharmacies through Health Information Exchange and Consent Manager (HIE-CM). While the foundational infrastructure is operational, adoption among healthcare providers is still growing.
04-health-records.notes: Over 500 million ABHA numbers have been created, but active linking of health records remains limited. ABDM is part of the broader India Stack digital public infrastructure. The system faces challenges in onboarding the vast private healthcare sector, which accounts for approximately 70% of healthcare delivery in India. DHIS2 is separately used for public health programme reporting.

05-population-registry.summary: India's National Population Register (NPR) is a comprehensive database of usual residents, collected under the Citizenship Act, 1955 and maintained by the Office of the Registrar General and Census Commissioner. The NPR was last updated during Census 2011 and a subsequent update exercise in 2015. While the dataset exists and is linked to Aadhaar numbers, there is no citizen-facing digital portal for individuals to view or update their population register records. The data primarily serves administrative and planning purposes rather than functioning as a real-time digital population register.

sources.title: Office of the Registrar General & Census Commissioner - NPR

05-population-registry.notes: The NPR update and Census 2021 exercises were delayed due to COVID-19 and have faced further postponements. While Aadhaar provides a de facto digital identity register, it is technically an identity verification system rather than a population register. The lack of an updated, real-time, citizen-facing digital population register distinguishes India from countries like Estonia or Finland.

06-social-protection.summary: India operates several large-scale digital social protection programs linked through the Aadhaar identity system. PM-KISAN provides direct benefit transfers to over 110 million farmers. Jan Dhan Yojana has enabled 500 million+ bank accounts for financial inclusion. The Public Distribution System (PDS) uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication for subsidized food grain distribution. However, significant gaps remain in rural connectivity and digital literacy, leading to exclusion errors.

sources.title: Jan Dhan Yojana - Department of Financial Services

06-social-protection.notes: The JAM trinity (Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile) forms the backbone of India's digital social protection delivery. Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) platform has disbursed over INR 30 lakh crore across 300+ schemes. Rural connectivity and biometric authentication failures remain challenges in remote areas.

07-tax-administration.summary: India operates a comprehensive digital tax administration system through the Income Tax e-Filing portal and the Goods and Services Tax Network (GSTN). The e-Filing portal handles over 70 million income tax returns annually with Aadhaar-based verification. GSTN processes all GST compliance for over 14 million registered businesses, including return filing, input tax credit matching, and e-invoicing. The Faceless Assessment and Appeals scheme conducts tax proceedings entirely online.
07-tax-administration.notes: The new e-Filing portal launched in 2021 consolidated multiple tax services into a single platform. Pre-filled returns with data from Form 26AS and Annual Information Statement (AIS) have simplified compliance. E-invoicing is mandatory for businesses above INR 5 crore turnover. TDS and TCS returns are fully digital.

08-land-property-registry.summary: India's land administration is managed by individual states, resulting in highly variable levels of digitization. The central Digital India Land Records Modernization Programme (DILRMP) aims to computerize land records, integrate registration and cadastral data, and enable online access. Several states like Karnataka (Bhoomi), Andhra Pradesh, and Maharashtra have made significant progress with online land records and digital mutation processes. However, many states still have incomplete or fragmented digital records.
08-land-property-registry.notes: DILRMP targets computerization of all land records across 36 states and union territories. The National Generic Document Registration System (NGDRS) enables online registration in participating states. Progress varies dramatically: some states have near-complete digitization while others have barely begun. Survey and cadastral map digitization remains a major challenge. The lack of conclusive titling (most states use presumptive titling) limits the effectiveness of digital systems.

09-education-platform.summary: India operates DIKSHA (Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing), built on the Sunbird open-source platform, serving as the national digital education infrastructure. The platform has reached over 300 million users across all states and union territories, providing QR-coded textbooks, digital courses, teacher training modules, and assessment tools in multiple Indian languages. The National Education Policy 2020 has further accelerated digital learning initiatives through platforms like SWAYAM for higher education and the National Digital Education Architecture (NDEAR).
09-education-platform.notes: DIKSHA/Sunbird is recognized as a Digital Public Good and has been adopted or explored by several other countries. The platform supports content in 36 Indian languages. Academic Bank of Credits (ABC) provides digital credential management for higher education. The PM eVIDYA initiative consolidated multiple digital education channels during COVID-19 and continues to operate.

11-electoral-services.summary: India provides digital electoral services through the National Voters' Service Portal (NVSP), which allows citizens to search the electoral roll, apply for new voter registration, make corrections, and track application status. The Voter Helpline App offers mobile access. Election results are published digitally by the Election Commission of India.
11-electoral-services.notes: NVSP supports Aadhaar-based verification for voter registration. India does not offer internet voting; voting is conducted via Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) at polling stations. The Voter Helpline App provides booth-level information and queue status.

12-justice-legal-aid.summary: India has made progress in digitizing court services through the e-Courts project, which provides online case status tracking, cause lists, and court orders for district and high courts. However, digital legal aid services remain limited, with the National Legal Services Authority (NALSA) offering basic online information but limited digital application processes.
12-justice-legal-aid.notes: The e-Courts project covers over 18,000 courts across India and provides case information via web and the e-Courts Services mobile app. The Supreme Court and some High Courts offer e-filing. Legal aid digitization under NALSA is progressing but remains incomplete, particularly at the district level.

13-agricultural-extension.summary: India offers several digital agricultural services including the e-NAM (National Agriculture Market) platform for online commodity trading, the Kisan Suvidha app for weather, market prices, and crop advisory, and the Soil Health Card portal. However, coverage is patchy, with significant gaps in rural connectivity and digital literacy limiting effective reach.

sources.title: Kisan Suvidha App - Ministry of Agriculture

13-agricultural-extension.notes: e-NAM connects over 1,000 mandis (agricultural markets) across India. The PM-KISAN scheme uses digital infrastructure for direct benefit transfers to farmers. The mKisan platform provides SMS-based crop advisories to millions of farmers, supplementing app-based services.

15-immigration-travel.summary: India provides digital immigration and travel services through the Passport Seva portal for passport applications and tracking, the e-Visa system for eligible foreign nationals, and the Bureau of Immigration's online services. However, processing still requires in-person visits for verification and biometrics, and some services experience delays and accessibility issues.

sources.title: Passport Seva - Ministry of External Affairs

15-immigration-travel.notes: The Passport Seva Project operates through a network of Passport Seva Kendras (PSKs) and Post Office Passport Seva Kendras for document verification and biometrics. India's e-Visa facility covers over 160 countries for tourism, business, medical, and conference purposes. The Immigration, Visa and Foreigners Registration & Tracking (IVFRT) system manages immigration checkpoints digitally.

02-civil-registration.summary: Kenya is rolling out an electronic Civil Registration and Vital Statistics (eCRVS) system through the Department of Civil Registration Services. The system digitizes birth, death, and marriage registration, and integrates with the national identification system (Huduma Namba). While urban registration coverage is relatively high, rural and remote areas still face significant gaps in both access and registration completeness.
02-civil-registration.notes: Birth registration completeness in Kenya is estimated at around 75%, with significant urban-rural disparities. The eCitizen portal allows online applications for birth certificates, but initial registration often still requires in-person visits. Kenya's Vital Statistics Act (2012) provides the legal framework for the system.

03-digital-payments.summary: Kenya is a global leader in mobile money, anchored by Safaricom's M-Pesa platform, which has over 30 million active users. M-Pesa enables person-to-person transfers, bill payments, savings, and loans via mobile phone without requiring a bank account. The government has integrated M-Pesa and other mobile money platforms for tax collection (iTax), government-to-person payments, and social protection disbursements. The Central Bank of Kenya regulates mobile money under the National Payment System Act.

sources.title: Central Bank of Kenya - National Payment System

03-digital-payments.notes: M-Pesa launched in 2007 and now processes transactions equivalent to approximately 50% of Kenya's GDP. Mobile money has been transformative for financial inclusion, with over 80% of adults using mobile money services. Kenya's success has served as a model for mobile money deployments across Africa and beyond.

06-social-protection.summary: Kenya's Inua Jamii programme provides cash transfers to orphans and vulnerable children, older persons, and persons with severe disabilities. The programme uses a digital single registry and increasingly leverages mobile money (M-Pesa) for payment delivery. The Enhanced Single Registry integrates beneficiary data across social protection programs. Digital registration is expanding but coverage remains incomplete in remote and arid areas.

sources.title: Inua Jamii - Ministry of Labour and Social Protection
World Bank - Kenya Social Protection Overview

06-social-protection.notes: Inua Jamii reaches approximately 1.2 million beneficiaries across three cash transfer programmes. Mobile money payments via M-Pesa have significantly improved delivery efficiency. The National Safety Net Program (NSNP) supported by the World Bank continues to strengthen digital systems, but targeting and enrollment in arid and semi-arid counties remain challenging.

09-education-platform.summary: Kenya has made notable progress in digital education through the Kenya Education Cloud and the Kenya Institute of Curriculum Development (KICD) digital content platform. The Kenya Education Cloud provides online learning resources aligned with the Competency-Based Curriculum (CBC). The KICD Kenya Education Cloud hosts digital textbooks, video lessons, and interactive content. The NEMIS (National Education Management Information System) manages student enrollment and school data digitally. However, device access and internet connectivity remain barriers in rural areas.
09-education-platform.notes: The Digital Literacy Programme (DLP) distributed devices to primary schools but sustainability and maintenance remain challenges. KUCCPS manages university placement digitally. The Kenya National Examinations Council (KNEC) has digitized exam registration and results processing. Private sector platforms like Eneza Education and M-Shule provide mobile-based learning that reaches students without internet access via USSD and SMS.

13-agricultural-extension.summary: Kenya has a growing ecosystem of digital agricultural extension services, including private platforms like M-Farm and Safaricom's DigiFarm, alongside the government's Kenya Agricultural and Livestock Research Organization (KALRO) portal. These provide market price information, agronomic advice, and input access, but coverage and adoption remain uneven across regions.

sources.title: KALRO - Kenya Agricultural and Livestock Research Organization

13-agricultural-extension.notes: DigiFarm leverages M-Pesa's mobile money network to reach smallholder farmers with financial services, inputs, and market access. KALRO provides research-based extension content. The National Agricultural Extension Policy promotes digital delivery, but county-level extension services remain largely in-person.

05-population-registry.summary: The Netherlands operates the Basisregistratie Personen (BRP), a fully digital population register managed by the Rijksdienst voor Identiteitsgegevens (RvIG) under the Ministry of the Interior. The BRP contains personal data for all residents of the Netherlands and Dutch citizens abroad, including identity data, addresses, family relations, nationality, and travel documents. Municipalities maintain and update records, which are shared in real-time with authorized government organizations through a centralized system.
05-population-registry.notes: The BRP replaced the older Gemeentelijke Basisadministratie (GBA) system in 2014. Citizens can view their registered personal data through the MijnOverheid portal using DigiD authentication. The BRP is one of several key base registers (basisregistraties) that form the backbone of Dutch digital government, following the once-only data collection principle.

08-land-property-registry.summary: The Kadaster (Netherlands' Cadastre, Land Registry and Mapping Agency) operates a fully digitized land registry system. All property transactions, mortgage registrations, and cadastral data are accessible online. The PDOK (Publieke Dienstverlening Op de Kaart) platform provides open geospatial data. The Kadaster's online portal enables professionals and citizens to search property records, view cadastral maps, and access ownership information digitally.
08-land-property-registry.notes: The Kadaster has been a leader in digital land administration for decades. All parcels in the Netherlands are registered with complete digital records. The organization actively contributes to international land administration standards and provides technical assistance to developing countries. Open data initiatives make much cadastral information freely available.

01-digital-identity.summary: Nigeria's National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) administers the National Identification Number (NIN) system, which is increasingly becoming a digital identity platform. The NIN is linked to biometric data and is required for SIM registration, banking, and government services. However, significant coverage gaps remain, with enrollment not yet reaching the full population, and digital verification services are still maturing.
01-digital-identity.notes: Nigeria mandated NIN-SIM linkage in 2020, which drove a surge in enrollments. As of 2025, over 100 million NINs have been issued, but the population of approximately 220 million means substantial gaps remain, particularly in rural areas. The digital verification service (NIN Verification) is operational but not yet universally integrated across government services.

05-population-registry.summary: Nigeria does not operate a comprehensive digital population register. While the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) maintains a National Identity Database linked to the NIN (National Identification Number), this functions primarily as an identity verification system rather than a population register with comprehensive demographic, address, and family relationship data. The National Population Commission (NPC) conducts periodic censuses but does not maintain a continuously updated digital population register.
05-population-registry.notes: Nigeria's last completed census was in 2006, though a new census has been planned. The NIN database, while growing, does not constitute a population register in the traditional sense as it lacks continuous address updates, family linkages, and real-time vital event integration. There is no citizen-facing portal for viewing or updating population register records.

10-business-registration.summary: The Corporate Affairs Commission (CAC) of Nigeria has digitized business registration through its online portal. The Company Registration Portal (CRP) enables name reservation, company incorporation, and post-incorporation filings. The introduction of the CAC mobile app and integration with the Bank Verification Number (BVN) system have improved accessibility. However, system reliability issues, intermittent downtime, and delays in processing continue to affect user experience. Physical visits are still sometimes required for certain filings.
10-business-registration.notes: The CAC waived registration fees for small businesses (Business Names) under the CAMA 2020 reforms to encourage formalization. The system supports registration of companies, business names, and incorporated trustees. Integration with FIRS (Federal Inland Revenue Service) for automatic TIN issuance has improved but is not always seamless. Payment gateway issues and document upload failures are commonly reported user complaints.

11-electoral-services.summary: Nigeria's Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) provides online voter registration through its CVR (Continuous Voter Registration) portal and publishes election results on the IReV (INEC Result Viewing) portal. However, many electoral processes remain paper-based, and digital infrastructure faces challenges with connectivity and accessibility in rural areas.
11-electoral-services.notes: INEC introduced the Bimodal Voter Accreditation System (BVAS) for biometric verification at polling units. The IReV portal was deployed for the 2023 general elections to improve results transparency, though technical challenges were reported during rollout.

12-justice-legal-aid.summary: Nigeria has minimal digital justice and legal aid services. The Legal Aid Council of Nigeria provides limited online information but does not offer digital application or case management. Court processes remain predominantly paper-based across federal and state courts, with very limited electronic filing capabilities.
12-justice-legal-aid.notes: Some pilot digitization efforts have been undertaken, including case management systems in select courts in Lagos and Abuja. However, these remain limited in scope. The Administration of Criminal Justice Act (ACJA) monitoring committee has recommended broader court digitization, but implementation is slow.

13-agricultural-extension.summary: Nigeria offers some digital agricultural services through the Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development (FMARD), including the e-Wallet system for distributing subsidized inputs (seeds and fertilizers) to farmers via mobile phones. However, digital extension services remain limited, with most advisory services delivered in person by state-level agricultural development programmes.

sources.title: Federal Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development (FMARD)
Nigeria Incentive-Based Risk Sharing System for Agricultural Lending (NIRSAL)

13-agricultural-extension.notes: The e-Wallet programme was launched in 2012 under the Growth Enhancement Support (GES) scheme, reaching millions of farmers for subsidized inputs. NIRSAL provides digital credit risk assessment for agricultural lending. Private platforms such as FarmCrowdy and Thrive Agric offer digital agricultural services but are not government-led.

14-public-procurement.summary: Nigeria has introduced the NOCOPO (Nigeria Open Contracting Portal) and the Bureau of Public Procurement (BPP) maintains an online portal for procurement information. However, adoption of e-procurement remains incomplete, with many agencies still relying on manual processes. The transition to fully digital procurement is ongoing.
14-public-procurement.notes: The Public Procurement Act 2007 established the BPP and introduced procurement reforms. NOCOPO was launched to improve transparency using open contracting principles. However, compliance with e-procurement requirements varies across federal ministries, departments, and agencies, and state-level procurement digitization is even more limited.

14-public-procurement.summary: South Korea operates KONEPS (Korea ON-line E-Procurement System), widely regarded as one of the world's most advanced e-procurement platforms. KONEPS handles the entire procurement lifecycle digitally, from tender announcement through bidding, evaluation, contracting, and payment, serving all central and local government agencies.

sources.title: KONEPS - Korea ON-line E-Procurement System (Public Procurement Service)

14-public-procurement.notes: KONEPS was launched in 2002 and processes procurement worth tens of billions of dollars annually. It integrates with over 200 external systems including business registration, tax, and banking. KONEPS has been recognized by the UN and World Bank as a best-practice model for e-procurement and has been replicated in several developing countries.

04-health-records.summary: Rwanda has deployed several digital health information systems, including DHIS2 for national health data aggregation and reporting, and OpenMRS for electronic medical records at select health facilities. The Rwanda Health Management Information System (R-HMIS) collects data from health centres and hospitals. While health facility reporting is largely digitized through DHIS2, patient-facing digital health records are not yet widely available, and electronic medical records are deployed at a subset of facilities.
04-health-records.notes: Rwanda is a leader in digital health adoption among low-income countries. DHIS2 is used for routine health reporting across all districts. OpenMRS has been deployed at referral hospitals and select health centres. Rwanda's community health worker programme (approximately 45,000 CHWs) uses RapidSMS and other mobile tools for community-level health data collection.

07-tax-administration.summary: The Rwanda Revenue Authority (RRA) operates an e-tax system that enables online filing and payment of major taxes including income tax, VAT, and customs duties. The system has achieved significant digitization for formal sector taxpayers, with electronic billing machines (EBMs) mandated for VAT-registered businesses. However, adoption among small and informal sector businesses remains limited, and digital literacy constraints affect usage rates outside major urban centers.
07-tax-administration.notes: RRA has made strong progress in digitizing tax administration, with the e-tax portal handling the majority of formal sector filings. Integration with Irembo (the national e-government platform) has improved accessibility. Mobile-based tax payment options via MTN Mobile Money and Airtel Money have expanded reach. The tax-to-GDP ratio continues to improve as digital systems broaden the tax base.

10-business-registration.summary: The Rwanda Development Board (RDB) provides online business registration through its integrated portal. Company registration can be completed online within 6 hours, making Rwanda one of the fastest countries in Africa for business incorporation. The system handles company name reservation, registration, tax identification number assignment, and social security registration in a single digital process. Rwanda's business registration reforms have been recognized as a model for the region.
10-business-registration.notes: Rwanda's Irembo e-government platform integrates business registration with other government services. The one-stop-shop approach consolidates multiple registration steps that previously required visits to different agencies. Rwanda has consistently improved its business environment rankings. The system also enables online registration for domestic companies, foreign branches, and cooperatives.

08-land-property-registry.summary: Samoa has deployed the Solutions for Open Land Administration (SOLA) system, a Digital Public Good, to manage land registration and administration. The system handles freehold and government land records digitally, supporting title registration, cadastral mapping, and land dispute resolution. However, approximately 81% of land in Samoa is held under customary tenure, which presents unique challenges for digital registration. The SOLA deployment covers formal land tenure while customary land management processes remain largely traditional.

sources.title: Samoa Ministry of Natural Resources and Environment
SOLA - Solutions for Open Land Administration

08-land-property-registry.notes: SOLA was originally developed with FAO support and is recognized as a Digital Public Good. Samoa was one of the first countries to deploy SOLA for land administration. The system has been adapted for the Pacific Island context. The challenge of integrating customary land tenure with formal digital registration remains an ongoing policy and technical issue across Pacific Island nations.

03-digital-payments.summary: Sweden is one of the world's most cashless societies, with digital payments deeply embedded in both private and public sectors. Swish, a mobile payment system developed by major Swedish banks, is used by over 8 million people (approximately 80% of the population). BankID provides secure digital authentication for government and financial transactions. The Swedish Tax Agency (Skatteverket) and other government bodies accept digital payments for taxes, fees, and fines through integrated online platforms.
03-digital-payments.notes: Cash usage in Sweden has declined to under 10% of point-of-sale transactions. The Riksbank is exploring an e-krona (central bank digital currency) as a complement to declining cash use. BankID, while not a payment system itself, is the authentication backbone for virtually all digital payments and government services in Sweden.

01-digital-identity.summary: GOV.UK One Login is replacing the legacy GOV.UK Verify system as the UK's primary digital identity platform. It enables citizens to prove their identity online to access approximately 45 government services, including tax filing, driving licence applications, and benefits claims. The system supports multiple identity verification methods including passport, driving licence, and biometric checks.
01-digital-identity.notes: GOV.UK Verify was officially retired in April 2023. One Login has been progressively onboarding services since 2022, with approximately 45 services integrated by early 2026.

02-civil-registration.summary: The United Kingdom offers comprehensive digital civil registration services. In England and Wales, births and deaths can be registered online through GOV.UK, with certificates available digitally. The General Register Office (GRO) maintains central records. Scotland and Northern Ireland operate separate but equally digitized registration systems. Marriage registrations are managed locally but feed into centralized digital records.
02-civil-registration.notes: England and Wales have had compulsory birth and death registration since 1837. The system has been progressively digitized, with online appointment booking, digital record searches, and electronic certificate ordering now standard. Registration completeness is effectively 100%.

03-digital-payments.summary: The United Kingdom has comprehensive digital payment infrastructure for government services. GOV.UK Pay provides a centralized payment platform used by over 400 government services for taxes, fines, licences, and applications. Benefits and pensions are paid via direct bank transfer (BACS). The Faster Payments Service enables real-time bank transfers. HMRC accepts digital payments for income tax, VAT, and corporation tax through multiple channels.
03-digital-payments.notes: GOV.UK Pay is an open-source platform developed by the Government Digital Service (GDS). It processes billions of pounds in government payments annually. The UK also has one of the highest card payment penetrations in the world, with over 85% of transactions being cashless.

04-health-records.summary: The United Kingdom provides digital health records through the NHS App, which enables patients in England to view their GP medical records, test results, vaccination status (including COVID-19), and prescriptions. Patients can also book appointments, order repeat prescriptions, and access NHS 111 online. Scotland (NHS Near Me), Wales (NHS Wales App), and Northern Ireland operate separate but comparable digital health platforms. Hospital records are increasingly accessible through linked systems.
04-health-records.notes: The NHS App has over 30 million registered users. NHS England's Federated Data Platform is being rolled out to improve data sharing across trusts and care settings. While GP records are broadly accessible digitally, hospital and specialist records integration remains a work in progress in some areas.

06-social-protection.summary: The UK provides comprehensive digital social protection through Universal Credit, which consolidates six legacy benefits into a single online system. Claimants manage applications, report changes, and receive payments digitally. The benefits checker on GOV.UK enables citizens to determine eligibility across all welfare programs. Additional digital services include Housing Benefit, Personal Independence Payment, and State Pension online claims.

sources.title: Department for Work and Pensions Digital Services

06-social-protection.notes: Universal Credit has been fully rolled out across the UK since 2024, replacing legacy benefits. Digital-by-default design with assisted digital support for those unable to use online services. Over 6 million households manage claims through the online journal system.

07-tax-administration.summary: HM Revenue and Customs (HMRC) provides comprehensive digital tax services. Self Assessment tax returns are filed online by over 12 million taxpayers annually. Making Tax Digital (MTD) mandates digital record-keeping and quarterly submissions for VAT-registered businesses and is expanding to Income Tax Self Assessment. The Personal Tax Account enables individuals to manage tax codes, check National Insurance records, and claim refunds online.
07-tax-administration.notes: Making Tax Digital for VAT has been mandatory since April 2022 for all VAT-registered businesses. MTD for Income Tax Self Assessment is being phased in from April 2026 for those with qualifying income over GBP 50,000. HMRC app allows mobile access to tax information and services.

08-land-property-registry.summary: HM Land Registry provides comprehensive digital services for property registration in England and Wales. The online portal enables title searches, property ownership verification, and digital submission of applications. Over 87% of land in England and Wales is registered. The Digital Registration Service allows conveyancers to submit applications electronically, and the Search for Land Property Information service provides instant online access to title registers and plans.

sources.title: Search the Register - HM Land Registry

08-land-property-registry.notes: HM Land Registry processes over 30 million search requests annually. The Digital Street research programme explores emerging technologies including digital identity verification for property transactions. Scotland and Northern Ireland operate separate land registries (Registers of Scotland and Land and Property Services respectively) with their own digital platforms.

09-education-platform.summary: The UK provides extensive digital education services across multiple platforms. The National Careers Service offers online career guidance, skills assessments, and course search tools. UCAS provides fully digital university admissions processing for over 700,000 applicants annually. Schools across England use the Department for Education's digital services for performance data, school comparisons, and teacher services. The Oak National Academy provides free online curriculum resources, and the National Tutoring Programme operates digitally.
09-education-platform.notes: The UK education system is highly digitized with schools using various management information systems (MIS) for attendance, assessment, and reporting. The Get Information About Schools (GIAS) service provides a comprehensive digital register. Student finance applications for England are fully digital through Student Finance England. Scotland, Wales, and Northern Ireland have their own education digital platforms.

10-business-registration.summary: Companies House provides fully digital company registration and management services. A new company can be registered online in as little as 24 hours for GBP 12. The digital service handles incorporation, annual filings, confirmation statements, and company updates. Over 99% of company incorporations are now filed digitally. The Companies House public register provides free online access to information on over 5 million companies, including filing history and officer details.
10-business-registration.notes: Companies House has been a pioneer in open data, providing a free public API and bulk data downloads. The Economic Crime and Corporate Transparency Act 2023 introduced identity verification requirements for directors and persons with significant control. Companies House is undergoing significant digital transformation to support its expanded role in combating economic crime.

11-electoral-services.summary: The UK offers comprehensive digital electoral services including the Register to Vote online service, the ability to check voter registration status, and access to election results through the Electoral Commission website. Postal and proxy vote applications are also available online.
11-electoral-services.notes: Register to Vote launched in 2014 and has processed tens of millions of applications. The service is consistently rated as one of the best GOV.UK digital services.

12-justice-legal-aid.summary: The UK provides comprehensive digital justice and legal aid services including the Legal Aid Eligibility Checker on GOV.UK, online money claims (small claims), online divorce applications, and the Find a Court or Tribunal service. The HMCTS (HM Courts & Tribunals Service) Reform Programme has digitized many court processes.

sources.title: Check if you can get legal aid - GOV.UK

12-justice-legal-aid.notes: The HMCTS Reform Programme has introduced online services for civil money claims, divorce, probate, and social security appeals. The Find a Court or Tribunal tool provides location, contact, and jurisdiction information for all courts in England and Wales.

13-agricultural-extension.summary: The UK provides comprehensive digital agricultural services through DEFRA's (Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs) online platforms, including the Rural Payments service for subsidies and land management, the Farming and Countryside Programme, and AHDB (Agriculture and Horticulture Development Board) market intelligence and research resources.

sources.title: AHDB - Agriculture and Horticulture Development Board

13-agricultural-extension.notes: The Rural Payments Agency handles Environmental Land Management (ELM) schemes, Basic Payment Scheme applications, and countryside stewardship online. DEFRA's Farming and Countryside Programme is transitioning agricultural support to digital-first delivery following the UK's departure from the EU Common Agricultural Policy.

14-public-procurement.summary: The UK offers fully digital public procurement through Contracts Finder for lower-value opportunities and Find a Tender for higher-value contracts above procurement thresholds. The Government Commercial Function oversees procurement policy, and the platform supports end-to-end digital tendering, contract award notices, and supplier registration.
14-public-procurement.notes: Find a Tender replaced the EU's OJEU/TED for UK procurement after Brexit. The Procurement Act 2023 introduced further reforms to digitize and simplify public procurement. The UK publishes procurement data in the Open Contracting Data Standard (OCDS) format.

15-immigration-travel.summary: The UK provides comprehensive digital immigration and travel services through GOV.UK, including online passport applications and renewals, visa applications via the UK Visas and Immigration portal, immigration status checking and sharing (View and Prove service), and ePassport gates at airports for automated border control.

sources.title: Apply online for a UK passport - GOV.UK

15-immigration-travel.notes: The UK has moved to a digital-by-default immigration system, with eVisas replacing physical immigration documents. The View and Prove service allows immigration status holders to share their status with employers and landlords digitally. The EU Settlement Scheme was a major digital-first immigration application.

06-social-protection.summary: The United States provides social protection through a fragmented system of federal and state programs. Benefits.gov serves as a federal eligibility screening tool across 1,000+ programs. Social Security Administration (SSA) offers online account management for retirement, disability, and survivor benefits. SNAP, Medicaid, and TANF are administered at the state level with varying degrees of digital service availability. The lack of a unified national system creates significant inconsistencies in digital access.
06-social-protection.notes: The US social protection digital landscape is characterized by federalism, with each state operating its own eligibility and enrollment systems for programs like SNAP, Medicaid, and unemployment insurance. Some states offer fully digital enrollment while others still require in-person visits. Login.gov is gradually being adopted as a shared identity layer. The fragmented approach results in a patchwork of digital maturity across the country.

07-tax-administration.summary: The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) provides extensive digital tax administration services. E-file processes over 150 million returns annually with a 90%+ electronic filing rate. The IRS Online Account allows taxpayers to view balances, payment history, and transcripts. The Direct File pilot program enables eligible taxpayers to file federal returns directly with the IRS for free. Free File partnerships provide no-cost tax preparation for qualifying individuals.
07-tax-administration.notes: The IRS Direct File program expanded from its 2024 pilot to cover additional states and tax situations. ID.me identity verification is used for secure account access. Modernization efforts under the Inflation Reduction Act have accelerated digital service improvements, though the system still relies heavily on third-party tax preparation software for most filers.

09-education-platform.summary: The United States has extensive digital education infrastructure at federal and state levels. The Federal Student Aid portal (FAFSA) processes over 17 million financial aid applications annually online. Ed.gov provides centralized access to federal education resources, data, and program information. The National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) offers comprehensive digital data tools. K-12 schools widely use digital learning management systems, and higher education institutions have robust online learning infrastructure.
09-education-platform.notes: The FAFSA Simplification Act streamlined the financial aid application to fewer questions. Education is primarily managed at state and district levels, leading to varied digital maturity. Major learning management systems (Canvas, Google Classroom, Schoology) are nearly universally adopted in K-12 and higher education. The digital divide in rural and low-income communities remains a concern despite federal broadband initiatives.

11-electoral-services.summary: The US federal government provides vote.gov as a centralized portal directing citizens to state-level voter registration. However, electoral administration is decentralized across 50 states, with wide variation in digital service availability. Some states offer full online voter registration and ballot tracking, while others require paper-based processes.
11-electoral-services.notes: As of 2026, approximately 42 states plus the District of Columbia offer online voter registration. Availability of digital services such as ballot tracking, sample ballot preview, and polling place lookup varies significantly by state and county. There is no internet voting at the federal level.

15-immigration-travel.summary: The United States offers extensive digital immigration and travel services including USCIS online account for immigration applications and case tracking, the State Department's travel.state.gov for passport applications, and Customs and Border Protection's Global Entry and Mobile Passport Control programs for expedited entry.
15-immigration-travel.notes: USCIS has expanded online filing to cover many immigration benefit forms. The ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization) allows Visa Waiver Program travelers to apply online. CBP One app provides mobile functionality for traveler processing. Online passport renewal was expanded in recent years.
